user_query:
I need help organizing outputs on my computer for a small belt collaboration and social content planning. Please use only the files under input/ and create the outputs under outputs/ as specified. Do not modify input files and create any missing folders under outputs/.

1) Meeting notes and action items
- Source: input/meeting/partner_call_notes.md
- Deliverables:
  a) outputs/meeting/meeting_notes.md containing: Title, Date, Attendees, Summary (3–6 bullets), Decisions (bullet list), and an Action Items table (columns: Item, Owner, Due, Status set to Open).
  b) outputs/meeting/action_items.csv with columns: item, owner, due, source_line (source_line is the original line text of the action from the input file). The CSV must include every action item you listed in meeting_notes.md.

2) Rewrite two Instagram captions for tone and brevity
- Source: input/social/draft_captions.md
- Deliverable: outputs/social/rewritten_captions.md with two sections titled "Caption 1" and "Caption 2". For each, rewrite in a friendly, clear, and concise tone (≤220 characters each). Preserve product names and all hashtags exactly as written.

3) Extract structured inspiration data from saved web content
- Source: input/web/inspiration.html
- Deliverable: outputs/web/inspiration_extracted.csv with one row per <article class="inspo-card">. Columns: title, designer, leather, technique, palette, date_iso (from the time@datetime attribute), tags (semicolon-delimited, in the order they appear). The number of rows must equal the number of .inspo-card elements in the HTML.

4) Filter, sort, and rank community comments
- Source: input/engagement/comments.csv
- Deliverable: outputs/engagement/top_comments.csv containing the top 5 comments after:
  - Case-insensitive filter: text contains either "strap" or "patina".
  - Keep only rows with likes >= 5.
  - Sort by likes (descending), then saves (descending), then timestamp (ascending) for tie-breaking.
  - Columns in the output: comment_id, author, likes, saves, text.

Everything should be reproducible from these inputs without internet access. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
